description_long: This is a brass wind instrument with a coiled, circular body and a large, flared bell at one end. The instrument features three rotary valves with white valve caps and levers, arranged in a row along the top of the tubing. The tubing is intricately looped and connected, forming a compact and elegant structure typical of orchestral horns. The bell is wide and gradually flares outward, while the mouthpiece is attached to a gently curved leadpipe extending from the main body. The surface is predominantly a golden brass color with contrasting white components for the valves and mouthpiece. The instrument's craftsmanship and detailing suggest it is designed for both visual appeal and acoustic performance.
description: A golden brass wind instrument with a wide flared bell, three white rotary valves, and a coiled, looping body. The mouthpiece and valve levers are white, contrasting with the main brass structure.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full circular coil, three rotary valves, and the wide bell opening.
description_view_1: Angled rear view highlighting the depth of the bell and the alignment of the leadpipe and mouthpiece.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the looping tubing and the bell's flare, with valves and mouthpiece visible.
description_view_3: Direct bell view showing the wide, circular opening and the instrument's internal flare.
attribute_bell_flare: Wide and gradually flared bell with a smooth, circular opening.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three rotary valves arranged in a row along the top of the instrument, each with a white lever.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: Gently curved leadpipe extending from the mouthpiece to the main body.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: Main tuning slide is U-shaped, located near the leadpipe and before the valve section.
